mechanism: Probabilistic inference over latent dimensions of social perception. Given visual salience of body versus face, update beliefs about mental capacities along two orthogonal dimensions (agency and experience) via Bayesian-like redistribution, not uniform de-mentalization. Perception shifts agent-related traits inversely with experience-related traits.
distribution: outcome: shifts in trait attribution along two latent dimensions; assumed: orthogonal structure of agency/experience

mechanism: Integration of consumption into autobiographical memory and self-concept. Experiences, once enacted, persist as episodic memories autobiographically linked to self; possessions reside in external space. Tighter integration correlates with greater satisfaction via self-serving bias and reduced regret.

Abstract concepts activate sensorimotor simulations of corresponding physical states. Chronic psychological burden (secret) activates embodied metaphor circuit matching physical load; perception and action scale accordingly as if weighted.

Shift between experience-based and theory-based judgments. When own knowledge biased by recent exposure, detect contamination and switch to analytic evaluation of item difficulty; faster theory-based judgments improve relative but not absolute accuracy.

Concept activation shapes belief system via symbolic association. Salience of money primes free-market principles (self-sufficiency, transactional relations, market-based allocation); beliefs then justify existing inequality as fair outcome of market forces.

Biased self-perception drives congruent behavioral display. High self-perceived competence generates behaviors signaling confidence (more speech, assertive tone, relaxed posture). Observers use these behavioral cues to infer competence; perception generalizes beyond actual ability.
